name: Manuscript Literature Framing
description: Draft LaTeX Introduction and Related Work from confirmed manuscript intent and Topic Synthesis evidence.

prompts.common: Use manuscript-literature-framing for paperTitle={{ parameter.paperTitle }}, language={{ parameter.language }}, targetVenue={{ parameter.targetVenue }}, articleType={{ parameter.articleType }}, stylePreference={{ parameter.stylePreference }}. This skill is a gate-driven staged workflow, not a direct drafting prompt. If a required Zotero or Synthesis host call is unavailable during real execution, run the legal cancel action with reason/message/paperTitle so the runtime writes manuscript-literature-framing.result.json. Use package-local scripts/gate_runtime.py, scripts/stage_runtime.py, scripts/runtime_state.py, and references/scientific_introduction_related_work_writing_guide_zh.md. Every normal step starts with gate and follows only its next_action and command_example; the only allowed bypass is cancel when required host calls are unavailable. Scripts only gate, validate, persist state, create runtime views, write final assets, and write the business fallback result file manuscript-literature-framing.result.json; all semantic work is done by the LLM. Stage script stdout is only an action receipt, not final assistant output. Stage 1 clarifies and confirms manuscript intent: research problem, target object/scenario, method/system, main contributions, target venue/style, whether Related Work is separate, and preferred organization mode. Stage 2 collects materials: call synthesis.list_topics, recommend 1-5 Topic Synthesis topics, confirm material scope, then read synthesis.get_review_input for confirmed topics. Use synthesis.get_reference_sidecar_index, synthesis.get_citation_graph_metrics, synthesis.get_citation_graph_slice, synthesis.resolve_topic_paper_digest, list_library_items/search_items, item/note/payload lookup, and prepare_paper_reading_context only when needed to support citekeys, evidence checks, or ambiguity resolution. Stage 3 performs multi-angle framing analysis before any writing plan: domain/route taxonomy, timeline/timeliness, gap-to-contribution alignment, and framing synthesis. Stage 4 builds and confirms a structured writing plan; before drafting it, read references/scientific_introduction_related_work_writing_guide_zh.md. The plan must cover the Introduction functional chain, Related Work taxonomy/method lines/benchmark dimensions/debates, whether survey-of-surveys is needed, evidence/citation candidates, topic provenance, and contribution alignment. Stage 5 is final drafting: the LLM composes Introduction and Related Work LaTeX from the confirmed writing plan, evidence inventory, and framing analysis; this is not template rendering or evidence concatenation. Submit the authored draft through persist_final_draft so the script can write assets and manuscript-literature-framing.result.json. Use Zotero citekeys as \cite{zotero_citekey}; never fabricate missing citekeys, use % TODO citation: paper_ref and diagnostics instead. Final assets are result/introduction.tex, result/related-work.tex, result/intent-brief.json, result/evidence-inventory.json, result/framing-analysis.json, result/writing-plan.json, result/citation-map.json, and result/diagnostics.json. Do not hand-write or modify result/result.json; ACP runner writes that file after final assistant envelope validation. Do not put __SKILL_DONE__ in manuscript-literature-framing.result.json or assets/output.schema.json. At final completion or cancellation, read manuscript-literature-framing.result.json and output exactly one JSON object containing __SKILL_DONE__: true plus all business fields from that file, with no explanations and no Markdown fences. Final result kind must be writing.manuscript_literature_framing for completed runs or manuscript_literature_framing_canceled for canceled runs.
